title: Documentation State
organization: Two Marshalls Studios — Studio Operating System (TMS-OS)
summary: Records the verified state, control rules, and remaining recovery work for permanent studio documentation.

sections.title: Purpose
paragraphs: This document is the permanent control record for the current state of Two Marshalls Studios documentation. It records what has been verified, what has already been published into the Google Headquarters structure, what still requires historical recovery, and the rules that prevent documentation from becoming fragmented or dependent on chat history.

sections.title: Core Rule
paragraphs: No documentation item is considered permanently established until its content, identity, destination, and status have been verified.

TMS-OS and StudioDB govern structured studio records. The approved Google Headquarters structure is the permanent human-readable documentation layer. GitHub preserves source code and project history. Chat, screenshots, temporary files, and local exports may provide evidence, but they must not become the only location of important studio knowledge.

sections.title: Verified Google Headquarters Structure
paragraphs: The permanent top-level Studio Headquarters structure has been created and verified:

sections.title: Verified Permanent Documents Currently Established
paragraphs: The following documents have been created, uploaded, converted to Google Docs where applicable, and visually verified during the current controlled population process:

rows: Records the current operating state and checkpoint of TMS-OS.
Defines the required bug investigation, correction, and retest sequence.
Defines how completed work becomes permanent structured documentation.
Preserves enhancement ideas from the beginning of the project without interrupting approved foundation work.

sections.title: The 16-Document Documentation Package
paragraphs: Historical recovery identified a verified core documentation package containing 16 permanent documentation categories. These are categories of required human-readable records, not a verified numbered upload sequence.

The existence of these 16 categories does not mean that all 16 Google documents have already been populated. Each category must be recovered, reconciled, created in the correct form, placed in the correct folder, and verified.

sections.title: Current Population Status
paragraphs: At this checkpoint, five permanent human-readable documents have been verified in the Google Headquarters structure. The broader 16-category package remains under controlled recovery and population.
items: Enhancement history baseline: Established and preserved in ENH-LOG-001
Automatic Google publishing: Deferred enhancement; not yet active

sections.title: Historical Recovery Requirement
paragraphs: Documentation must be reconstructed from the earliest available Two Marshalls Studios project history. Recovery must not begin only with the current StudioDB architecture or the current chat.
The historical reconstruction must preserve:
items: Bugs, root causes, fixes, and tests
Enhancement ideas from the beginning of the project

rows: Governs structured studio records and relationships.
Preserves source code, commits, versions, and project history.
Recovery evidence and working assistance; not a permanent sole source of truth.
Screenshots, local files, ZIPs, and exports
Supporting evidence and transfer material until reconciled into permanent records.

sections.title: Documentation Control Process
paragraphs: Every permanent document must follow this sequence:
1. Identify the verified source material.
2. Determine the correct permanent document type and ID.
3. Reconcile duplicate, conflicting, or outdated information.
4. Create the complete formatted document.
5. Place it in the approved Google Headquarters folder.
6. Convert it to a Google Doc when required.
7. Verify title, content, location, and visible formatting.
8. Remove temporary duplicate upload files after conversion.
9. Update documentation state and index records.
10. Preserve any newly identified enhancement ideas without interrupting current work.

sections.title: Current Risks
items: Mistaking documentation categories for a fixed numbered upload sequence.
Assuming a document is populated because its category exists in a backup.
Losing historical reasoning by documenting only current outcomes.
Allowing important information to remain only in chat.
Creating duplicate Google and Word copies after conversion.
Building enhancements during foundation or recovery work without explicit approval.
Failing to capture enhancement ideas from the beginning of the project.

sections.title: Current Priority
paragraphs: Complete the human-readable Google documentation layer without creating a competing source of truth.

The controlled sequence is to continue recovering the verified core documentation package, create each permanent document in the approved format, place it in the correct Headquarters folder, verify it, and update the documentation state as the package becomes complete.

sections.title: Next Controlled Documentation Action
paragraphs: After DOC-STATE-001 is uploaded, converted, and verified, the next document must be selected from the remaining verified documentation categories based on recovery priority and available source evidence. No assumed numbering sequence should be used.

sections.title: Completion Checklist
items: 16 documentation categories distinguished from upload sequence

sections.title: Permanent Statement
paragraphs: The Two Marshalls Studios documentation system must preserve the complete history of the studio from the beginning of the project forward. Permanent documentation is built through verified recovery, controlled publication, and continuous session closeout—not by relying on memory, chat history, or a single workstation.

revisionHistory.summary: Connected the verified permanent document to the shared structured Document Template Engine.

verification.method: Converted from the recovered DOCX source used to create the verified Google Headquarters document.
